name: TCS
description: India's largest IT consultant, focusing heavily on foundations, string manipulation, and core mathematical algorithms.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Array Traversals & Basic Loops - Master linear iterations, prime factor checks, and basic math logics. | Week 2: String Parsing & Palindromes - Practice string reversals, character counts, and anagram matchings. | Week 3: Searching & Sorting Foundations - Master Binary Search and basic Bubble/Selection sorting algorithms. | Week 4: Technical MCQs & Core CS - Review final year projects, SQL joins, and basic OOP principles.
interviewProcess: Cognitive & Aptitude Assessment: Automated test covering numerical ability, verbal skills, and basic logic. | NQT Coding Assessment: Solve two easy-to-medium coding problems primarily focused on array operations or string transformations. | Technical Interview: Discussion on final year projects, computer network basics, database joins, and coding dry runs. | HR & Cultural Interview: Final fitment round evaluating communication skills and flexibility.

faqs.question: What is the typical coding difficulty in TCS NQT?
faqs.answer: The coding section features Easy to lower-Medium difficulty questions focusing on fundamental math, array manipulations, and string processing.

faqs.question: How many rounds does the TCS selection process have?
faqs.answer: Typically 3-4 rounds: Online assessment (Aptitude + Coding), Technical Interview, and final HR interview.

interviewExperiences.title: TCS Digital Candidate
questionsAsked: Check if a number is a prime palindrome
Find the second largest element in an array in a single pass
interviewExperiences.tips: Focus heavily on logic building. Explain your brute force approach before writing code.

companyRoadmap.focus: Master core traversals, prime checking, and basic arithmetic logic.
Practice string reversal, character indexing, and basic anagram matching.
Implement binary search boundaries and in-place swaps cleanly.
Simulate mock tests using last year's TCS NQT coding sets.

name: Infosys
description: IT giant targeting array manipulation, hash map lookups, and basic binary tree structures during recruitment tests.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: List Traversals & Hash Maps - Master prefix sums, subarray totals, and key element lookups. | Week 2: String Matching & Two Pointers - Solve character frequency arrays and duplicates detection. | Week 3: Basic Data Structures - Review Singly Linked List operations and pointer movements. | Week 4: DBMS & OOPs Foundations - Review standard SQL queries, OOP principles, and class designs.
interviewProcess: Online Assessment (HackWithInfy / InfyTQ): Automated assessment featuring coding questions ranging from Medium to Hard. | Technical Screen Interview: In-depth discussion on database normalization, SQL queries, coding logic, and project details. | HR Assessment: Managerial behavioral assessment and communication evaluation.

faqs.question: What is the coding pattern in Infosys SP/DSE assessments?
faqs.answer: Infosys Specialist Programmer (SP) and Digital Specialist Engineer (DSE) assessments focus on Medium to Hard topics like Trees, Graphs, and Dynamic Programming.

faqs.question: How to prepare for Infosys technical interview?
faqs.answer: Focus heavily on DBMS, SQL queries, OOPs, and basic DSA concepts like Linked List reversals and Binary Search.

interviewExperiences.title: Infosys DSE Candidate
questionsAsked: Given a list of strings, find all anagram pairings
Reverse a Linked List recursively
interviewExperiences.tips: Explain SQL joins and normalization concepts thoroughly when asked. They love database fundamentals.

companyRoadmap.focus: Master prefix sums, subarray totals, and key element lookups.
Solve character frequency arrays and duplicates detection.
Review Singly Linked List operations and pointer movements.
Tackle Infosys specific sample tests and timed coding mocks.

name: Wipro
description: Global IT consultant assessing basic variables, sorting algorithms, and string searching bounds.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Math & Basic Recursion - Review basic recursive logic, prime factors, and looping setups. | Week 2: Linear Traversals & Indexing - Practice scanning arrays, boundary checks, and string indexing. | Week 3: Standard Searching & Sorting - Be comfortable implementing Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Linear Search. | Week 4: Networking & Operating Systems - Brush up on TCP/IP layers, process states, and OS basic concepts.
interviewProcess: Wipro Elite NTH Assessment: Automated exam containing basic aptitude, verbal, and 2 easy coding questions on platform. | Technical Interview: Reviewing core programming knowledge, live dry running, and project structure. | HR Interview: Basic communication and cultural suitability check.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty of Wipro Elite NTH coding questions?
faqs.answer: Generally Easy. Questions are simple arithmetic, basic array updates, or standard string validations.

faqs.question: Are there separate technical and HR interviews in Wipro?
faqs.answer: Yes, Wipro uses a 2-stage interview process: a Technical interview covering basic coding + projects, followed by an HR interview.

interviewExperiences.title: Wipro Elite Candidate
questionsAsked: Check for valid parenthesis
Fibonacci series using recursion
interviewExperiences.tips: Focus on clean coding style. Be prepared to answer basic OOP concepts like inheritance and polymorphism.

companyRoadmap.focus: Review recursion basics, prime factors, and basic loop setups.
Practice scanning arrays, index bounds, and string indexing.
Be comfortable implementing Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Linear Search.
Solve high-frequency previous Wipro Elite coding questionnaires.

name: Accenture
description: Leading consultant targeting dynamic programming basics, arrays, and standard recursion loops.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Contiguous Array Processing - Practice scanning segments, array partitioning, and index sums. | Week 2: String Parsing & Anagrams - Review anagram frequencies, string parsing, and character tracking. | Week 3: Hashing & Logic - Master Binary Search and basic hashing keys logic. | Week 4: Software Engineering Concepts - Review SDLC models, database structures, and testing types.
interviewProcess: Cognitive and Technical Assessment: Automated test measuring cognitive capability and basic tech skills. | Timed Coding Test: Solve 2 coding problems focusing on arrays, strings, or sliding windows. | Technical & HR Interview: Discussion about resume projects, OOPs, web technologies, and candidate profile.

faqs.question: Does Accenture have elimination rounds in its OA?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Cognitive and Technical section is an elimination round. If cleared, you immediately start the coding assessment.

faqs.question: What is the typical difficulty of Accenture coding rounds?
faqs.answer: Usually one Easy question (e.g. array manipulation) and one Medium question (e.g. bitmasking or basic DP/Greedy).

interviewExperiences.title: Accenture ASE Candidate
questionsAsked: Binary search in a sorted array
Remove consecutive duplicate characters from a string
interviewExperiences.tips: Brush up on computer networking, Operating Systems, and software engineering terms before the technical interview.

companyRoadmap.focus: Practice scanning segments, array partitioning, and index sums.
Review anagram frequencies, string parsing, and character tracking.
Master Binary Search and basic hashing keys logic.
Practice MCQ sections alongside mock coding papers.

name: Capgemini
description: Assesses array indexing, sorting techniques, and basic hash table implementations.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Pseudocode & Logic Trace - Practice trace tables, bitwise shifts, and conditional conditional loops. | Week 2: Array Swaps & Segments - Review array operations, sorting bounds, and swaps. | Week 3: String Palindromes & Rotations - Solve basic palindrome matching and compression tricks. | Week 4: Project Discussion & OOPs - Review database models, Java OOPs concepts, and project diagrams.
interviewProcess: Game-Based Assessment: Online interactive games evaluating analytical behavior and memory. | Pseudocode & Coding Test: Solve analytical pseudocode dry runs and 1-2 coding problems. | Technical Interview: Reviewing code logic, OOPs, project tech stack, and systems. | HR Interview: Career discussions and cultural fitment.

faqs.question: What is Capgemini's Pseudocode round?
faqs.answer: It is a highly conceptual test of basic code debugging, loop dry runs, and time complexity checks in the initial filter stage.

faqs.question: How difficult are Capgemini's coding questions?
faqs.answer: Generally Easy to lower-Medium. Most focus on string parsing, basic math structures, and simple sorting algorithms.

interviewExperiences.title: Capgemini Analyst Role
questionsAsked: Check if string is a rotation of another
Find all pairs in array with a given target sum
interviewExperiences.tips: Practice game-based assessment formats beforehand as they are unique. In interviews, stay calm and express key thoughts.

companyRoadmap.focus: Practice trace tables, bitwise shifts, and conditional outcomes.
Review array operations, sorting bounds, and swaps.
Solve basic palindrome matching and compression tricks.
Complete 3 Capgemini mock exams under tight visual timers.

name: Cognizant
description: Recruits through tests analyzing linked list operations, binary search peaks, and sliding windows.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Linked List Operations - Master Singly/Doubly Linked list insertions, pointer shifts, and mergers. | Week 2: Monotonic Stacks & Queues - Practice basic stack pushes, parenthesis validations, and next greater elements. | Week 3: Binary Search Peaks - Master binary search bounds, rotations, and element detections. | Week 4: Relational DB & SQL - Write queries, review DBMS architecture, index locks, and schema definitions.
interviewProcess: Aptitude & Technical OA: Initial assessment testing verbal capability, analytical logic, and database MCQs. | Coding Assessment (GenC Elevate): Solve 2 coding problems primarily on sliding windows, array algorithms, or string manipulation. | Technical Interview: Deep dive into DSA logic, live code writing, database models, and SQL joins. | HR Alignment: Behavioral suitability and document verification.

faqs.question: What is the Cognizant GenC Developer coding test like?
faqs.answer: The test features Easy-Medium coding puzzles (primarily array/string traversals) along with pseudocoding MCQs.

faqs.question: Does Cognizant focus on DB questions?
faqs.answer: Yes. Basic database schemas, SQL operations (joins, aggregates), and indexing are frequently asked in the interview.

interviewExperiences.title: Cognizant GenC Elevate Candidate
questionsAsked: Merge two sorted arrays in-place
Find next greater element using a stack
interviewExperiences.tips: Tackle OOPs, basic structures, and SQL queries first. They highly value database normalization answers.

companyRoadmap.focus: Solve basic subarray checks, sum ranges, and elements insertion.
Check for unique character limits and anagram frequency grids.
Review standard SQL queries, table locks, and key references.
Tackle sample timed papers designed for Cognizant recruitment tests.

name: Amazon
description: E-commerce giant prioritizing advanced graphs, dynamic programming, priority queues, and behavioral criteria.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Heaps & Monotonic Stacks - Master sliding window extremes, priority queues, and LRU Cache structures. | Week 2: Graph DFS/BFS Traversals - Practice topological sort, cycle detection, and shortest pathways on acyclic graphs. | Week 3: Dynamic Programming Arrays - Formulate state equations, bottom-up matrix allocations, and knapsack optimizations. | Week 4: Amazon Leadership Principles - Structure past accomplishments using the STAR model mapped to Amazon's 16 LPs.
interviewProcess: Online Assessment: Solve two medium-to-hard coding problems followed by a comprehensive system work simulation. | Technical Interview I (Coding & LPs): Solve algorithmic problems while explaining dynamic complexity under Leadership Principles. | Technical Interview II (Advanced DSA): Live whiteboarding on advanced tree properties, graph paths, or complex Dynamic Programming. | Bar Raiser Interview: High-level architectural, algorithmic, and cultural evaluation focused on LP alignment.

faqs.question: How important are Leadership Principles (LPs) at Amazon?
faqs.answer: Extremely critical. Almost 50% of the interview score relies on how well your past projects demonstrate Amazon's 16 Leadership Principles.

faqs.question: What is the typical coding difficulty in Amazon interviews?
faqs.answer: Medium to Hard. Focus is heavily on sliding window, heaps, priority queues, graphs (Dijkstra/Topological Sort), and Dynamic Programming.

interviewExperiences.title: Amazon SDE-1 Candidate
questionsAsked: Sliding Window Maximum
Course Schedule (Topological Sort)
interviewExperiences.tips: For every answer, describe how you applied specific leadership principles. Structure your behavioral examples using the STAR method.

companyRoadmap.focus: Solve complex sliding window, prefix metrics, and key lookups.
Master pointer operations, LRU Cache setup, and next greater arrays.
Practice depth searches, topological sort steps, and shortest paths.
Review Amazon's 16 Leadership Principles alongside dynamic grids.

name: Google
description: Global technology leader assessing highly efficient complex segment trees, advanced graphs, and recursion.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Segment Trees & Fenwick - Master range query structures, segment manipulations, and tree updates. | Week 2: Advanced Graph Algorithms - Deep dive into Dijkstra, Union Find disjoint sets, and network flow matrices. | Week 3: Bitmask DP & State-Space - Practice state reductions, memory optimizations, and matrix pathfinding. | Week 4: Googleyness & Case Studies - Study team collaboration, scalability design, open-ended problem solving, and mock dry-runs.
interviewProcess: Technical Phone Screen: 45-minute coding round on a Google Doc assessing coding cleanliness, naming, and complexity explanation. | Onsite Coding Round I: Advanced graph networks, topological pathways, or tree node balancers. | Onsite Coding Round II: State-space Dynamic Programming, segment trees, or custom mathematical arrays. | Googleyness & Leadership Round: Assessing cultural fitness, inclusive decision making, and navigation of ambiguous situations.

faqs.question: What does Google look for in coding rounds?
faqs.answer: They evaluate your problem-solving process, boundary checks, efficiency (time/space complexity), and communication skills. They expect clean code.

faqs.question: Does Google ask about standard templates?
faqs.answer: Rarely. Google coding questions are unique, highly customized, and frequently combine multiple topics (e.g. Tree traversals + DP).

interviewExperiences.title: Google SWE Candidate
questionsAsked: Implement a custom scheduler with priorities and dependencies
Longest Path in a Directed Acyclic Graph
interviewExperiences.tips: Do not rush to code. Spend the first 10 minutes discussing your approach, edge cases, and time/space trade-offs with the interviewer.

companyRoadmap.focus: Master prefix lookups, segment splits, and sliding intervals.
Tackle topological sort, DFS pathways, and tree nodes balancer.
Formulate dynamic state spaces and bottom-up matrices logic.
Master Segment Trees, Union Find, and Dijkstra's paths.

name: Microsoft
description: Technology pioneer focusing on tree nodes, AVL balancer operations, DFS/BFS graph scans, and heaps.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Linked Lists & Pointers - Master reversing pointers, detecting loops, and fast/slow pointer traversals. | Week 2: AVL Trees & Balance - Review depth searches, tree balance validations, and lowest common ancestor logic. | Week 3: DFS & BFS Graph Scans - Practice connectivity tracking, matrix traversals, and bidirectional path searches. | Week 4: System Design Architecture - Study microservices, message queues, relational vs NoSQL, and scalability bottlenecks.
interviewProcess: Codility Online Assessment: Automated screening test on Codility containing 3 medium coding questions focused on edge cases. | Technical Onsite Round I: Live coding focusing on array partitions, string parsing, or custom stack behaviors. | Technical Onsite Round II: Custom tree structures, recursively traversing nodes, and algorithmic design. | System Design Interview: Design large-scale distributed systems, discussing caching, databases, and scaling principles.

faqs.question: What is the focus of Microsoft Codility OA?
faqs.answer: The online assessment usually consists of 3 coding questions of Medium difficulty to be solved in 120 minutes on Codility.

faqs.question: Does Microsoft ask system design questions?
faqs.answer: Yes. In SWE interviews, they frequently spend one full round evaluating system design patterns (e.g. designing WhatsApp or a URL shortener).

interviewExperiences.title: Microsoft SDE-1 Candidate
questionsAsked: Merge intervals recursively
Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
interviewExperiences.tips: Focus on array, string, and tree structures. Microsoft places high value on clean, bug-free implementations of classical algorithms.

companyRoadmap.focus: Solve subarray modifications, key frequency tracking, and swaps.
Reversing list pointers, detecting loops, and swapping nodes.
Review depth searches, tree balance limits, and key nodes.
Tackle sample timed papers designed for Microsoft recruitment tests.

name: Adobe
description: Multimedia software giant evaluating stack evaluation, monotonic structures, and binary search ranges.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Monotonic Stacks & Buffers - Master stack-based lookups, bracket validation, and window maximum limits. | Week 2: BST Properties & Rotations - Practice balance checking, BST validation, and key element predecessor/successor searches. | Week 3: C++ Memory & Pointer Layouts - Review pointer arithmetic, stack/heap allocations, virtual inheritance, and reference structures. | Week 4: Core Utility & System Tools - Design file structure index mappings, custom heap memory managers, and timed mocks.
interviewProcess: HackerEarth Coding OA: Automated assessment featuring aptitude, core systems concepts, and 2 medium coding challenges. | Technical Round I (Pointers & Memory): In-depth questioning on heap/stack boundaries, pointer logic, and BST structures. | Technical Round II (Algorithms): Live code whiteboarding on monotonic stack properties, tree nodes, or graphs. | Technical & Managerial Round: Discussion about scalability design, project architecture, and organizational alignment.

faqs.question: What coding platforms does Adobe use?
faqs.answer: Adobe typically hosts its online coding assessments on HackerEarth or CoCubes.

faqs.question: What is the typical interview focus at Adobe?
faqs.answer: Adobe places emphasis on Operating Systems, Memory Management, C/C++ pointers, and classical DSA puzzles (Stack, Heap, BST).

interviewExperiences.title: Adobe MTS Candidate
questionsAsked: Validate standard BST properties for a tree
Next Greater Element using Monotonic Stack
interviewExperiences.tips: Practice explaining memory allocation concepts, pointer arithmetics, and virtual inheritance in C++ if applicable.

companyRoadmap.focus: Practice scanning segments, array partitioning, and index sums.
Master stack bounds, parenthesis validators, and heights tracker.
Review depth searches, tree balance checks, and paths.
Simulate mocks using last year's Adobe coding sets.

name: Deloitte
description: Professional services leader evaluating array manipulations, standard sorting algorithms, and SQL basics.
preparationTimeline: Week 1: Array Traversals & Basic Operations - Master scanning elements, duplicates mapping, and integer math checks. | Week 2: String Parsing & Palindromes - Practice clean string reversals, subsequence checking, and char counts. | Week 3: Database & SQL Normalization - Master standard database schemas, write SQL group by statements, and index joins. | Week 4: Analytical Puzzles & Case Prep - Practice structured case studies, resume project walkthroughs, and mock interviews.
interviewProcess: Online Assessment: Initial cognitive evaluation, relational database MCQs, and 1 easy coding challenge. | Technical Interview: Reviewing core programming knowledge, SQL joins, database normalization, and coding logic. | final Partner Interview: High-level managerial, behavioral alignment, and consulting case discussions.

faqs.question: What is the coding difficulty at Deloitte?
faqs.answer: Generally Easy to lower-Medium difficulty questions focusing on fundamental math, array manipulations, and string processing.

interviewExperiences.title: Deloitte Analyst Candidate
questionsAsked: Find duplicate elements in an array
Reverse a string without library functions
interviewExperiences.tips: Focus on logical programming, solid communication, and detailed resumes.

companyRoadmap.focus: Master core traversals, prime checking, and basic arithmetic logic.

name: Flipkart

preparationTimeline: Week 1: Sliding Window Boundaries - Master sliding window maximums, pointer movements, and index sums. | Week 2: Heaps & Priority Queues - Implement priority trees, solve K-way merges, and heapify arrays. | Week 3: Topological Sorting & DFS - Practice topological graphs, vertices paths, and depth-first searches. | Week 4: Machine Coding & LLD - Review object-oriented programming, design clean class diagrams, and practice timed low-level designs.
interviewProcess: Online Assessment: Automated assessment featuring 3 medium-to-hard coding problems on HackerEarth. | Machine Coding Round: Formulate clean, bug-free, low-level class models for a system (e.g. Splitwise) in 2 hours. | Technical Interview I (Algorithmic): Whiteboarding on graphs, dynamic programming, or trees. | HR & Managerial Interview: final cultural fitment, compensation discussions, and organizational matches.

faqs.question: What is the typical coding difficulty in Flipkart interviews?
faqs.answer: Medium to Hard. Focus is heavily on sliding window, heaps, priority queues, graphs, and Dynamic Programming.

questionsAsked: Sliding Window Maximum
Course Schedule (Topological Sort)
interviewExperiences.tips: Write modular, extensible code in the Machine Coding round. Follow solid SOLID design principles.

faqs.question: What is the typical coding difficulty in Walmart interviews?
